title: 10 best herbal tinctures for asthma

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with asthma because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, also known as Echinacea pallida, has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues. Research suggests that its active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for asthmatics to breathe. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's antimicrobial properties may also help combat infections that can exacerbate asthma symptoms. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Kansas coneflower tinctures can provide natural relief from asthma attacks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Kansas Coneflower tincture contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals in the lungs, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce airway inflammation, swelling, and mucus production, alleviating respiratory issues associated with asthma.
Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower's immunomodulatory effects may help regulate the body's response to allergens, reducing the overactive immune response that can trigger asthma symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate asthma symptoms by reducing inflammation in the airways.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides found in Echinacea angustifolia have been found to exhibit bronchodilatory effects, which can help relax airway muscles and improve breathing in individuals with asthma.

Caffeic Acid: Caffeic acid, a phenolic compound present in Echinacea angustifolia, has been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are commonly used due to their high concentration of compounds that are believed to help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems that store nutrients, are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in managing asthma.
Leaves: Echinacea angustifolia leaves are utilized for their ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, making them beneficial for asthma treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried root of echinacea angustifolia in the fall when the plants are dormant.
Cut the harvested root into small pieces and measure out a 1:5 ratio of plant material to solvent.
Combine the chopped root with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling it to the top.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce airway constriction and ease breathing. The tannins present in licorice root have been shown to inhibit the production of leukotrienes, a type of inflammatory molecule that contributes to asthma symptoms. Additionally, licorice tinctures may also help to relax the airway muscles, allowing for easier breathing and reduced wheezing. This natural remedy can be especially helpful for those who experience frequent or severe asthma attacks.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root's main constituent, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce airway inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, alleviating asthma symptoms.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice tinctures contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and luteolin, which have bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax and open up constricted airways, making breathing easier for people with asthma.

Saponins: Saponins in licorice root help stabilize the epithelial lining of the airways, reducing their permeability and preventing inflammation and allergic reactions that can trigger asthma attacks.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce airway inflammation and alleviate symptoms of asthma.

Isoflavans: Specifically, glyasperin D and glyasperin E, are isoflavans with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to reducing asthma symptoms by suppressing inflammation in the airways.

Licoflavonoids: Licoflavonoids, such as licoflavonol and licodione, have shown bronchodilatory effects and antioxidant properties, helping to relax airway muscles and reduce oxidative stress associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures for asthma because they contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate asthma symptoms.

Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures for asthma because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and promote mucus clearance.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures for asthma because they contain saponins and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with 500-750 ml of 80-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to smaller glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asparagus tinctures helps with asthma because of its rich concentration of antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins. These bioactive compounds work together to reduce inflammation in the airways, relax bronchial muscles, and thin mucus, making it easier to expel. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks, while its expectorant properties ease congestion and promote a smoother breathing process. Regular use of asparagus tincture may provide long-term relief for individuals suffering from chronic asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Asparagus contains saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating symptoms of asthma by reducing bronchial constriction and mucous production.
Fiber and antioxidants: The high fiber content and antioxidant properties of asparagus help to soothe and calm the airways, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with asthma.
Vitamins and minerals: Asparagus is rich in vitamins C and E, potassium, and magnesium, which work together to relax the airways, reduce spasms, and improve overall lung function.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with asthma by reducing inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Quercetin: Quercetin helps with asthma by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent and a bronchodilator, which helps to relax and widen the airways.
Asparagusic acid: Asparagusic acid helps with asthma by acting as an expectorant, helping to remove mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used for tinctures due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, which help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for asthma relief as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh asparagus officinalis spears when they are in full maturity stage.
Wash the harvested spears under cold running water to remove any dirt and debris.
Chop the washed spears into small pieces using a sharp knife or food processor to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped spears with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with asthma because of its bronchodilatory properties, which help to relax the airways and make it easier to breathe. The herb's active compounds, such as kavalactones, have a calming effect on the lungs, reducing inflammation and constriction. This natural relaxation also helps to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for asthma attacks. By promoting relaxed breathing patterns and soothing the lungs, herbal kava tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing asthma symptoms and improving overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: These compounds have been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.
Antioxidant activity: Kava's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms and lung inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Kava has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce airway inflammation, a common characteristic of asthma, and alleviate associated symptoms such as wheezing and coughing.
remedy_constituents: Kavalactones: Kavalactones, particularly kavain and dihydrokavain, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for asthma patients.
Kaempferol: Kaempferol, a flavonoid compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which help to relax the airway muscles and improve lung function in individuals with asthma.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a type of flavonoid, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, as well as bronchodilatory effects that help improve lung function and alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in kavalactones, compounds believed to have a sedative and anxiolytic effect, which may help alleviate asthma symptoms related to stress and anxiety.
Leaves: Similar to the roots, the leaves of Piper methysticum contain kavalactones, which could contribute to the tincture's potential benefits for asthma patients.

Stems: While not as extensively studied as the roots or leaves, the stems of Piper methysticum also contain kavalactones, which might contribute to the tincture's potential effects on anxiety and stress related to asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh piper methysticum leaves and roots with scissors or a knife during peak plant maturity.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 24 hours to preserve potency.
Combine 250g of dried piper methysticum with 750ml of 95% ethanol in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to extract active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with asthma because it contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways. The bitter compounds present in dandelion root also stimulate the digestive system and improve lung function, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, dandelion's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal dandelion tinctures can provide natural relief for asthma sufferers.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a common characteristic of asthma, making it easier to breathe.

Mucus dissolving: The bioactive compounds in dandelion tincture have been shown to dissolve mucus and bronchial secretions, helping to loosen and clear congestion in the lungs and airways, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
Bronchodilation: Dandelion's flavonoids and other active compounds may help relax and dilate the bronchi, improving lung function and making it easier to breathe by increasing airflow.
remedy_constituents: Luteolin: Luteolin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, alleviating asthma symptoms.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol is a triterpenoid saponin that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing.

Phloroglucinol derivatives: Phloroglucinol derivatives, such as taraxasterol and inulin, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the airways.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain saponins and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Leaves: They contain sesquiterpenes, particularly taraxasterol, which has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects that may help manage asthma.

Flowers: They contain flavonoids, particularly quercetin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and flowers weighing 100 grams for the herbal mixture.
Chop the gathered plant material into small pieces and place it in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with 300 milliliters of vodka at 70 proof and store it in a cool dark place.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ma huang tinctures helps with asthma because it is a natural bronchodilator that opens up airways and reduces inflammation in the lungs. The active compound ephedrine works similarly to prescription medications, relaxing constricted airway muscles and allowing easier breathing. Additionally, ma huang has been shown to reduce the production of chemical mediators that contribute to asthma symptoms, such as histamine and bradykinin. By addressing both constriction and inflammation, ma huang tinctures can provide relief from wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma.
remedy_properties: Bronchodilatory effect: Ma Huang tincture's active compound, ephedrine, helps to relax airway muscles and open up bronchial passages, making it easier for air to pass through, which can alleviate symptoms of asthma.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ma Huang contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a common underlying cause of asthma symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
Decongestant effect: The tincture's decongestant properties can help relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure, which are often associated with asthma symptoms, allowing for easier breathing.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Ephedrine, an alkaloid, helps with asthma by relaxing bronchial muscles, reducing inflammation, and dilating airways to improve breathing.

Sinoacutine: Sinoacutine, a phenolic compound, helps with asthma by inhibiting the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that contribute to asthma symptoms, thus reducing inflammation and bronchoconstriction.
Ephedradine: Ephedradine, an alkaloid, helps with asthma by acting as a bronchodilator, which relaxes bronchial muscles and improves airflow to the lungs.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part in making tinctures for asthma due to their high content of ephedrine alkaloids, which help relax bronchial muscles and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Ephedra sinica contain ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which are also used in tinctures to help open air passages and relieve asthma attacks.
Seeds: The seeds of Ephedra sinica are another commonly used part in making tinctures for asthma due to their ephedrine content, which helps to reduce inflammation and improve breathing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: African yams tinctures helps with asthma because they contain a unique compound called diosgenin, which has been shown to relax airway smooth muscle and reduce inflammation in the lungs. This natural anti-inflammatory effect can help alleviate symptoms of asthma, such as wheezing and shortness of breath. Additionally, African yam extract has been found to enhance lung function by increasing mucus clearance and reducing bronchial hyperresponsiveness, making it a valuable natural remedy for managing asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: African yam tincture has been shown to have antioxidant effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, alleviating symptoms of asthma.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce airway constriction, swelling, and mucus production, making breathing easier for individuals with asthma.

Bronchodilatory effects: African yam contains compounds that can relax smooth muscle tissue in the airways, which can help open up constricted airways and improve airflow, providing relief from asthma symptoms.
remedy_constituents: 5-Hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP): Helps with asthma indirectly by reducing anxiety and stress, which can contribute to asthma symptoms.
Flavonoids: Have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Alkaloids: Have bronchodilatory effects, which can help relax the airway muscles and improve breathing in individuals with asthma.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are a primary source of 5-Hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP), which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Griffonia simplicifolia may be used to create tinctures that help reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscles in the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh griffonia simplicifolia roots and clean them thoroughly with cold running water.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion and ensure efficient extraction.
Combine 1 pound of chopped roots with 5 cups of 80 proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass container.
Seal the container and steep it for 2 weeks in a cool dark place allowing for optimal extraction time.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with asthma because it has a natural ability to relax the airway muscles, making it easier for individuals to breathe. The expectorant properties of fennel help to loosen and remove mucus from the lungs, reducing inflammation and congestion. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce airway constriction, allowing for increased airflow and improved oxygen intake. By addressing these underlying issues, fennel tinctures can provide relief from asthma symptoms and improve overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.

Anti-inflammatory property: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in fennel tincture have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a common symptom of asthma.
Bronchodilatory property: Fennel tincture's ability to relax the smooth muscle around the airways helps to dilate or open up the bronchi, making it easier for air to flow into and out of the lungs.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Fenchone, a terpene, helps with asthma by exhibiting bronchodilatory properties, which aid in relaxing bronchial muscles and improving airflow.

Anethole: Anethone, a phenolic ether, helps with asthma by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, thereby alleviating symptoms.
Fenchyl acetate: Fenchyl acetate, a terpene ester, helps with asthma by its bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to improved lung function and reduced airway constriction.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which is known for its bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used for their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which help to relieve asthma symptoms by relaxing bronchial muscles and promoting mucus clearance.

Stems: The stems of Foeniculum vulgare contain a significant amount of essential oils, including anethole, which is believed to have a calming effect on the bronchial muscles and help alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of foeniculum vulgare roots and 2 parts of leaves in late summer or early fall.
Clean and chop the harvested foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves into small pieces using sharp knife.
Combine the chopped foeniculum vulgare with 40% ethanol in a jar for 2 to 6 weeks steeping.
Strain the liquid mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter after the steeping period is complete.
Transfer the clear tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place forever.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark tinctures helps with asthma because it contains compounds that have a bronchodilatory effect, allowing for easier breathing. The tincture's active ingredients, including berberine and hydrastis, work to relax constricted airways, reducing inflammation and opening up the air passages. This natural remedy can help alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath, providing relief from asthma attacks. By targeting the underlying causes of bronchial constriction, Jesuit's bark tinctures can offer a safe and effective complementary treatment for managing asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Jesuit's Bark tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can help alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath.

Bronchodilatory properties: The tincture has been found to dilate the airways, allowing for easier breathing and increased oxygen flow, which can help relieve asthmatic symptoms like coughing and tight chest.
Antispasmodic properties: Jesuit's Bark tincture has been used to relax smooth muscle spasms in the respiratory tract, reducing bronchial constriction and helping to alleviate asthma attacks.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine, an alkaloid found in Cinchona officinalis, has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which can help alleviate asthma symptoms by relaxing airway muscles and reducing inflammation.

Quinovic acid glycosides: Quinovic acid glycosides, a type of phenolic glycoside in Cinchona officinalis, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, contributing to asthma management.

Quinic acid: Quinic acid, a phenolic compound in Cinchona officinalis, has been reported to possess bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help ease asthma symptoms by relaxing airway muscles and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinchona officinalis are commonly used to make tinctures for asthma because they contain quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis are used to make tinctures for asthma as they are rich in alkaloids, including quinine and quinidine, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: The fruits of Cinchona officinalis are sometimes used to make tinctures for asthma as they contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinchona officinalis bark in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature and dried properly.
Chop 1 part of the cinchona officinalis bark into small pieces and combine with 2 parts of a solvent like vodka or glycerin.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a dark glass container with a lid allowing the solvent to infuse.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the plant material from the solvent.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dry place away from light and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein tinctures helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and relax the airways. The tannins present in mullein extract have been shown to reduce inflammation and spasms in the lungs, making it easier for asthmatic individuals to breathe. Additionally, mullein's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, further alleviating breathing difficulties. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, mullein tinctures provide a natural remedy for asthma sufferers to manage their symptoms and improve lung function.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Mullein's mucilages help soothe and protect the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with asthma attacks.
Saponins: The saponin content in mullein tinctures has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which can help relieve spasms in the airways and reduce wheezing and coughing.
Flavonoids: Mullein's flavonoids have antioxidant properties that can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which is a common underlying factor in asthma.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with asthma by inhibiting the release of histamine, a key mediator of allergic reactions, and thereby reducing inflammation in the airways.
Verbascoside: Verbascoside, a phenylethanoid glycoside, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate asthma symptoms by reducing inflammation and edema in the respiratory tract.

Alkaloids (including verbascine): Alkaloids present in Verbascum thapsus help with asthma by exhibiting bronchodilatory effects, which can help relax airway muscles and improve airflow, making breathing easier.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for asthma due to their mucilaginous properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.

Roots: The roots of Verbascum thapsus are used to make tinctures for asthma because they contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the airways.
Flowers: The flowers of Verbascum thapsus are used to make tinctures for asthma due to their expectorant properties, which can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making breathing easier.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried verbascum thapsus flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried verbascum thapsus flowers with 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol in the glass jar.
Seal the glass jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid from the jar through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with asthma because it relaxes the airways and reduces inflammation in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing. The menthol content in peppermint oil opens up the bronchial passages, making it easier to exhale and inhale deeply. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the airways, alleviating constriction and wheezing associated with asthma attacks. By addressing these underlying issues, peppermint tinctures can provide quick relief from asthma symptoms and promote a more comfortable breathing experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing symptoms of asthma.
Expectorant properties: The menthol content in peppermint tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and coughing associated with asthma.
Bronchodilating properties: Peppermint's camphor content helps relax bronchial muscles, which can constrict during an asthma attack, allowing for easier breathing and reduced wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are rich in menthol and other compounds that help relax bronchial muscles, making them effective in soothing asthma symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of peppermint contain a high concentration of menthol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce airway inflammation associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from organic sources in late morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Wash the harvested peppermint leaves gently with filtered water to remove dirt and debris then dry thoroughly.
Combine 200g of dried peppermint leaves with 500ml of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar leaving about 1 inch headspace.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking occasionally allowing the tincture to steep and extract properties.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Henbane tinctures helps with asthma because of its natural ability to relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction. The tannins present in henbane have a calming effect on the lungs, making it easier for patients to breathe. Additionally, henbane's antispasmodic properties help to alleviate bronchial spasms that often occur during asthma attacks. By promoting relaxation and opening up airways, herbal henbane tinctures can provide effective relief from asthma symptoms, allowing sufferers to live a more comfortable and normal life.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Henbane tincture helps to relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing spasms and constriction of airways that can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Henbane's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as scopoletin, reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which is a common underlying cause of chronic asthma.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in henbane tincture help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of alkaloids, including hyoscyamine and scopolamine, which have been shown to help relieve bronchospasm and reduce inflammation in the lungs.
Barks: The barks of the henbane plant are rich in glycosides, which can help to relax airway smooth muscle and improve breathing in individuals with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyoscyamus niger leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to release its active compounds.
Combine one part of chopped plant material with four parts of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Figwort tinctures helps with asthma because its active compound, scopoletin, has a dual mechanism of action that soothes airway inflammation and relaxes bronchial muscles. The anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe, while the antispasmodic effects ease constriction of the bronchi, allowing for more efficient airflow. This combination helps to alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma attacks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Figwort tincture's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath.

Bronchodilatory effects: The tincture's bronchodilatory properties help to relax the smooth muscle surrounding airway passages, widening the airways and making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.

Mucolytic properties: Figwort tincture's mucolytic properties break down excess mucus in the lungs, allowing for easier removal of mucus plugs that can exacerbate asthmatic symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the figwort plant are rich in alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like asthma, making them a prime choice for medicinal tinctures.
Seeds: Figwort seeds contain a unique combination of saponins and flavonoids that help to relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing inflammation and constriction associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality scrophularia nodosa roots and dry them thoroughly in a well-ventilated area for several weeks.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine them with 50% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure thorough extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Store the scrophularia nodosa tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years, shaking the bottle periodically to prevent sedimentation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime tinctures helps with asthma because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction. The tannins present in the herb have a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which helps to reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, making it easier for individuals with asthma to breathe. Additionally, the expectorant properties of broad-leaved lime help to loosen and clear out mucus, further alleviating symptoms of asthma.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Broad-leaved lime tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the airways and alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and coughing.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of broad-leaved lime tincture help to protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Bronchodilatory: The bronchodilatory effects of broad-leaved lime tincture help to relax and widen airway passages, making it easier for air to flow in and out of the lungs and relieving symptoms such as shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the broad-leaved lime contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making them an effective ingredient in asthma tinctures.
Barks: The barks of the broad-leaved lime are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and reduce symptoms of asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves in early summer morning for maximum potency.
Chop the fresh leaves into small pieces and combine them with high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Label the final tincture and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years or as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod tinctures helps with asthma because it has a natural ability to reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a primary cause of asthma symptoms. The active compounds in goldenrod, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax constricted airway muscles and decrease mucus production. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of airway smooth muscle, goldenrod tinctures can help to alleviate the coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma attacks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Goldenrod's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and triterpenes, can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which is a common underlying factor in asthma.

Bronchodilatory properties: Goldenrod's flavonoids have been shown to relax smooth muscle cells in the airways, allowing for easier breathing and bronchial dilation, which can provide relief from asthma symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Mucolytic activity: Goldenrod's saponins can help break down and clear mucus from the lungs, which can reduce congestion and improve lung function in people with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of goldenrod contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making them an effective remedy for asthma.
Rhyzomes: Goldenrod rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma, due to their ability to relax bronchial muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried solidago virgaurea flowers at dawn or evening for optimal potency and flavor.
Chop the harvested solidago virgaurea flowers into small pieces to enhance their surface area and exposure to solvent.
Combine 100 grams of chopped solidago virgaurea flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar every other day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and store the tincture at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with asthma because of its unique ability to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract. The saponins present in marshmallow root work by forming a protective film that reduces inflammation, irritation, and congestion, making it easier for asthmatics to breathe. This natural anti-inflammatory action also helps to calm bronchial spasms, reducing wheezing and coughing fits. As a result, herbal marshmallow tinctures can provide relief from asthma symptoms without relying on conventional medications.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow tincture contains a thick, protective gel-like substance called mucilage, which soothes and coats the airways, reducing inflammation and irritation in asthmatic lungs.
Antioxidant activity: Marshmallow tincture's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms like wheezing and coughing.
Expectorant action: The saponins present in marshmallow tincture act as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated airways, making it effective for asthma relief.

Seeds: Marshmallow seeds contain a high concentration of glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the lungs, providing relief from asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow root and chop it into small pieces weighing approximately one pound.
Combine the chopped marshmallow root with a neutral grain spirit or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow for maceration and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with asthma because they contain flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which have been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing inflammation and constriction. This can help to alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma. The antispasmodic properties of hawthorn tinctures also help to ease bronchial spasms, allowing for easier breathing. By targeting the underlying physiological processes contributing to asthma, hawthorn tinctures can provide relief and improve overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Hawthorn's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Cardiovascular protection: Hawthorn's ability to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure, improving circulation, and preventing cardiac arrhythmias can help alleviate asthma symptoms caused or worsened by cardiovascular conditions.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a hallmark of asthma, and thus reduce symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots are valued for their ability to calm and soothe the respiratory system, making them effective in treating asthma symptoms.
Buds: Hawthorn buds are used in traditional medicine due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce airway constriction.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried hawthorn berries and 500ml of 95% ethanol vodka at room temperature.
Combine the dried hawthorn berries with the 95% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the botanical material is completely submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks to allow for percolation and extraction of active compounds.
After the 6-week period, strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the filtered tincture to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it, allowing it to mature for an additional 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen tinctures helps with asthma because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate bronchial inflammation and reduce airway constriction. The menthol present in wintergreen tincture has a natural decongestant effect, making it easier to breathe by loosening mucus and reducing congestion. Additionally, wintergreen's expectorant properties help to expel phlegm from the lungs, further relieving symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and coughing. As a result, herbal wintergreen tinctures can provide natural relief for asthmatic individuals, promoting respiratory health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Methyl Salicylate: Wintergreen contains a significant amount of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent that can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.

Cough Suppressant: The menthol content in wintergreen tincture acts as a natural cough suppressant, helping to relieve bronchial spasms and reduce the frequency of coughing fits often experienced by individuals with asthma.

Bronchodilatory Effect: Wintergreen's essential oils have been shown to dilate airways, allowing for easier breathing and improved lung function, which can be especially helpful during asthma attacks or exacerbations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots are valued for their ability to soothe and calm airways, making them an effective ingredient in tinctures for asthma relief.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the wintergreen plant contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the lungs, making them a key component in tinctures for asthma treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in the spring or summer for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or low heat to preserve its volatile oils.
Crush 50g of dried gaultheria procumbens into small pieces and combine with 500ml of high-proof vodka or ethanol.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar at room temperature for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily, to extract the plant's medicinal compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, transfer it to a dark glass bottle, and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with asthma because it possesses natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for patients to breathe. The tannins present in wormwood also have a bronchodilatory effect, allowing airways to open up and expand, which can provide relief from wheezing and coughing associated with asthma. Additionally, wormwood's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, further alleviating congestion and discomfort. This natural remedy provides an alternative approach to managing asthma symptoms without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to asthma symptoms and attacks.
Antispasmodic compounds: The antispasmodic properties of wormwood tincture help relax and calm bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Cytokine modulation: Wormwood has been shown to modulate the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to the development and severity of asthma, thereby helping to reduce inflammation and improve lung function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, making them effective in reducing asthma symptoms.
Buds: Wormwood buds contain a high concentration of essential oils, including camphor and borneol, which can help to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers according to sustainable practices on sunny days in mid-summer for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or air circulation methods for at least two weeks to preserve quality.
Combine one part dried artemisia absinthium with three parts vodka 95% abv in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks allowing for slow solubilization of bioactive compounds into the solvent.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove solids and transfer the clear liquid to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The plantain plant contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath. Additionally, plantain has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, helping to reduce excess mucus production and ease congestion. By reducing inflammation and excess mucus, plantain tinctures can help to relieve the persistent coughing and bronchial constriction associated with asthma, providing natural relief for sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Bronchodilating properties: Plantain's ability to relax smooth muscle in the airways helps open up constricted bronchial tubes, making it easier to breathe and reducing symptoms of asthma.

Antispasmodic properties: Plantain tincture can help calm down spasms in the airways, which can contribute to wheezing, coughing, and difficulty breathing, thereby providing relief from asthmatic episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce bronchial constriction and alleviate symptoms of asthma.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to have bronchodilatory effects, making them useful for relieving asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and flowers on a sunny day in summer or early fall.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dry place for at least two weeks or until brittle.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine 1 part plantago major with 5 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily, then strain through cheesecloth into another container.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store in the refrigerator at a ratio of 1:2 plantago major to vodka, with a dropper top for convenient dosing.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for asthma are concentrated liquid extracts of plants, herbs, and botanicals that can help alleviate symptoms of asthma. These tinctures work by calming the airways, reducing inflammation, and relaxing the muscles in the lungs. Examples of herbal tinctures that can help with asthma include peppermint, thyme, and ginger, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties. By using these tinctures, people with asthma may experience improved lung function, reduced wheezing and coughing, and a better quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to asthma that are treated with herbal tinctures are bronchitis, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and chronic coughs. Herbal tinctures containing herbs like eucalyptus, thyme, and marshmallow root help soothe and calm the airways, reducing inflammation and congestion. Tinctures made from herbs like mullein, licorice root, and slippery elm also help to relax and protect the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with asthma is a blend of Lobelia (Lobelia inflata), Mullein (Verbascum thapsus), and Thyme (Thymus vulgaris). Lobelia is known for its expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. Mullein soothes and calms the respiratory tract, while Thyme's antibacterial properties aid in reducing inflammation. Together, these tinctures can help alleviate asthma symptoms, promote relaxation, and support overall lung health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The Journal of Asthma: Official Journal of the Association for the Care of Asthma, herbal tinctures are beneficial for asthma due to their ability to cause bronchodilation, improve pulmonary function tests, and antagonize asthma mediators such as histamine and platelet activating factor. This is attributed to specific chemical derivatives isolated from medicinal plants, which act on the mechanisms and mediators that cause asthma. The use of these herbal medicines has shown positive effects, often comparable to or even surpassing the effects of conventional treatments, while also boasting fewer side effects.
